title: Correct: u64 size limit saturates to usize::MAX on 32-bit targets
description: WHY THIS IS CORRECT: eval_size_test uses usize::try_from(l).unwrap_or(usize::MAX) to convert the u64 limit to usize. On 32-bit targets, limits exceeding u32::MAX saturate. For :over a saturated limit: message_size > usize::MAX is always false (correct — no real message can be larger than addressable memory). For :under a saturated limit: message_size < usize::MAX is always true (correct — a 10 GB size test on a 32-bit host is vacuously true, but no real message will be 10 GB on a 32-bit host). The comment in the code explains this correctly.
